---
format:
  typst:
    papersize: us-letter
    margin:
      x: 1.2cm
      y: 0.9cm
    fontsize: 9pt
    mainfont: "Helvetica Neue"
    include-in-header: _onepager-preamble.typ
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
source("../shiny/R/logic.R")
d <- uat_load("../templates")
s <- uat_summary(d$tests, d$defects, d$trace)
g <- uat_gate(d$tests, d$defects, d$trace)
verdict     <- gsub("_", "\\\\_", g$verdict)
verdict_col <- if (g$go) "#43c47d" else "#e8657a"
```

```{=typst}
#let muted = rgb("#94a1c0")
#let panel = rgb("#222630")
#let card(body) = block(fill: panel, radius: 6pt, inset: 8pt, width: 100%, body)

#text(size: 15pt, weight: "bold")[IRT / RTSM User Acceptance Testing]
#h(1fr)
#text(fill: muted, size: 8pt)[Cheat-sheet · operations-only · synthetic · no PHI · say *participant* not *subject*]

#line(length: 100%, stroke: 0.5pt + rgb("#3a4157"))
#v(2pt)

#grid(columns: (1fr, 1fr), gutter: 8pt,
  card[
    == What UAT confirms
    In a test environment you confirm the vendor's randomization and drug-supply build does *exactly* what your URS and specs say, before any real participant is randomized. You operate the staging IRT in a browser, capture dated screenshot evidence, and transcribe results into your workbook so the record is attributable and stands alone for an auditor.
  ],
  card[
    == The go-live gate (conjunctive)
    Sign-off is READY only when *all three* hold:
    #list(tight: true,
      [Every *Critical* test passes],
      [*0* open Critical / Major defects],
      [*100%* traceability (every requirement has a test)],
    )
    Snapshot now: #text(fill: rgb("`r verdict_col`"), weight: "bold")[`r verdict`]. \
    #text(fill: muted, size: 8pt)[Severity, not count, decides. The gate packages evidence; a named human signs.]
  ],
)

#v(3pt)

#grid(columns: (0.9fr, 1.1fr), gutter: 8pt,
  card[
    == Live snapshot
    #table(columns: (1fr, auto), stroke: none, inset: 2.5pt,
      [Test scripts], text(fill: rgb("#e9edf7"))[*`r s$total`*],
      [Passed], text(fill: rgb("#43c47d"))[*`r s$pass`*],
      [Failed], text(fill: rgb("#e8657a"))[*`r s$fail`*],
      [Open defects], text(fill: rgb("#e0a64c"))[*`r s$open_def`*],
      [Requirements traced], text(fill: rgb("#3fd0c9"))[*`r s$cov`%*],
    )
  ],
  card[
    == Defect severity drives the gate
    #table(columns: (auto, 1fr, auto), stroke: none, inset: 2.5pt,
      text(fill: rgb("#e8657a"))[*Critical*], [Could break the blind, mis-randomize, mis-dose, or corrupt integrity], text(fill: rgb("#e8657a"))[Blocks],
      text(fill: rgb("#e0a64c"))[*Major*], [Significant functional error in a key workflow (resupply, wrong kit, missing alert)], text(fill: rgb("#e0a64c"))[Blocks til fixed],
      text(fill: muted)[*Minor*], [Cosmetic / non-functional (label typo, missing site number)], text(fill: rgb("#43c47d"))[Fix or waive],
    )
  ],
)

#v(3pt)

#card[
  == Where it runs: the systems (the workbook is the paper trail)
  #grid(columns: (auto, auto, auto, auto, auto, auto, auto, auto, auto), align: horizon, gutter: 5pt,
    text(size: 8.5pt)[*Staging IRT* (vendor, test list)], text(fill: muted)[#sym.arrow.r],
    text(size: 8.5pt)[*Operate in a browser*], text(fill: muted)[#sym.arrow.r],
    text(size: 8.5pt)[*Dated screenshots + exports*], text(fill: muted)[#sym.arrow.r],
    text(size: 8.5pt)[*Excel workbook* (status·defects·traceability·gate)], text(fill: muted)[#sym.arrow.r],
    text(size: 8.5pt)[*eTMF* (signed package)],
  )
  #text(fill: muted, size: 8pt)[Failures also go to the *vendor defect tracker*; the vendor promotes a fixed build back to staging for retest. The workbook never connects to the IRT, you transcribe, on purpose.]
]

#v(3pt)

#grid(columns: (1fr, 1fr), gutter: 8pt,
  card[
    == Anatomy of an executable test (RAND-02)
    A good test has a *specific, verifiable* expected result, not "works correctly".
    #list(tight: true, spacing: 3pt,
      [*Precondition:* test list loaded; the \<70 kg stratum, block 1, has an unused slot.],
      [*Steps:* register 101-001 at 68.0 kg #sym.arrow.r randomize #sym.arrow.r record kit + confirmation #sym.arrow.r open the allocation report as the unblinded statistician.],
      [*Expected:* allocated to the next unused \<70 kg / block-1 entry; arm, sequence, kit match the list; the investigator sees a *kit ID only*, not the arm.],
      [*Verify:* compare cell-for-cell across every stratum and both arms, not one happy-path participant.],
    )
  ],
  card[
    == The 8-step workflow
    #grid(columns: (1fr, 1fr), gutter: 4pt,
      [*1.* Derive test cases from URS + specs (positive · negative · boundary)],
      [*2.* Load the approved test list into staging IRT],
      [*3.* Execute each case in the browser; be systematic],
      [*4.* Record result + dated screenshot evidence],
      [*5.* Log every failure as a defect with a severity],
      [*6.* Vendor fixes; retest the new build],
      [*7.* Traceability: every requirement maps to a passing test],
      [*8.* Evaluate the gate; a named human signs it into the eTMF],
    )
  ],
)

#v(3pt)

#card[
  == Run the interactive tool
  #grid(columns: (auto, 1fr), align: horizon + left, gutter: 10pt,
    raw("install.packages(c('shiny','bslib','DT','ggplot2'))\nshiny::runApp('uat_irt/shiny')", lang: "r", block: true),
    text(fill: muted, size: 8pt)[Select a test, mark it Pass/Fail, close a defect, and the KPIs + go-live gate recompute live. The gate math lives in `shiny/R/logic.R`, shared by the app, the guide, the slides, and the unit tests. Full source in the run-and-build page.],
  )
]
```
